questions.question: What is the key idea behind the Union-Find decoder?

options.text: Grow clusters around defects and merge them until all have even parity
Use neural networks to predict corrections

questions.explanation: The Union-Find decoder grows clusters around defects. When clusters meet, they merge. The algorithm continues until all clusters have even parity (an even number of defects), indicating a valid correction has been found.

questions.question: What is the time complexity of Union-Find operations with path compression and union by rank?

options.text: O(α(n)) where α is the inverse Ackermann function

questions.explanation: With path compression and union by rank, Union-Find operations take O(α(n)) time, where α is the inverse Ackermann function. This grows so slowly that it's essentially constant (≤5 for all practical n).

questions.question: How does the Union-Find decoder track when to stop growing a cluster?

options.text: When it reaches a fixed size
When its parity becomes even (even number of defects)
After a fixed number of rounds

questions.explanation: A cluster stops growing when its parity becomes even (it contains an even number of defects). Even parity indicates that the defects within the cluster can be matched among themselves.

questions.question: How does Union-Find compare to MWPM in terms of threshold?

options.text: Union-Find has a slightly lower threshold (~0.9% vs ~1.0%)
They have exactly the same threshold

questions.explanation: Union-Find has a slightly lower threshold (~0.9%) compared to MWPM (~1.0%). This small sacrifice in accuracy is offset by significantly better speed and real-time capability.

questions.question: What is the main advantage of Union-Find over MWPM?

options.text: Near-linear time complexity, better suited for real-time decoding
Works with more types of codes

questions.explanation: Union-Find's main advantage is its near-linear time complexity O(d² α(d²)), compared to MWPM's O(V³). This makes it much better suited for real-time decoding, especially at larger code distances.

questions.question: How does Union-Find handle odd-parity clusters that can't find another cluster to merge with?
questions.explanation: Virtual boundary clusters (with parity 0) at the code boundaries can absorb odd-parity clusters when they grow to reach the boundary. This handles errors that end at the boundary.

questions.question: The Union-Find decoder is highly parallelizable, allowing near-linear speedup with multiple processors.
questions.explanation: True. Different regions of the lattice can grow clusters independently in parallel, with merging only needed at region boundaries. This makes Union-Find excellent for parallel implementation on FPGAs or multi-core systems.
